messages.content: Explain about the kpi’s that alex spoke about today
Searching today’s lifelogs for mentions of Alex and KPIs...

Here’s what Alex set as KPIs today, and how he framed them:

- Headline KPI (the one metric to rally around): number of new patients who complete intake each month
  - Quote (Alex): “רצינו לבחור KPI שקל להתחבר אליו… מספר אנשים חדשים שעושים אינטייק בחודש.”
  - Baseline he used for August: 391 new intakes (from “510” total minus “119” follow-ups)
    - Quote (Alex): “510… אני מוריד את הפולואפים, את ה-119, ואני מגיע ל-391.”
  - Target and timeline: reach 660 new intakes by December/January via a linear month‑over‑month ramp
    - Quote (Alex): “בבסוף דצמבר ינואר אנחנו צריכים להיות ב-660… ההגעה מעכשיו לשם… בצורה ליניארית.”

- Supporting operational KPI: patient wait time (to case manager and to psychiatrist) as the capacity signal
  - Quote (Alex): “הדרך שלי לבדוק את זה זה זמן ההמתנה… זה ה-KPI הכי חשוב לוגיסטית.”

- How marketing/sales/ops tie into the KPI:
  - Marketing (Adi) to grow digital + PR; Field sales (Adiel) to build clinic referrals; Operations to staff/execute so wait times stay acceptable.
  - Alex stressed integrated pacing so demand growth matches clinic capacity and patient experience.

- Measurement details and attribution:
  - Marketing attribution via the “How did you hear about us?” question to apportion PR vs paid digital.
  - CRM/dashboards need to reflect these KPIs and updated payer logic (e.g., Maccabi follow-up payments) for accurate tracking.

- Additional KPIs raised in the discussion (by you) to track alongside the headline KPI:
  - Conversion: “how many we bring” vs “how many actually do intake”
  - Active patients at any time
  - Number of sessions
  - Financials: revenue (actual vs potential)

Want me to draft a one-page KPI dashboard spec (metrics, owners, data sources, and views) for Shachar/CRM so the team can implement this week?
